$comment: Expected canonical post-migration state for docs/save_compat/manifest.json's b2-split-haskell-lua-state fixture (issue #766 requirement 3: 'the independently split Haskell components from #760', round-7 review). Byte-identical Haskell-side content to c3-typed-reference-v1-minimal (same real building/unit/craft-bill/power-node/gen-params, extracted via decodeSessionEnvelope's already-modern path then re-encoded via World.Save.Component.encodeComponentSpecs) -- the ONLY difference is unit-sim/craft-bills/power-nodes are re-encoded at their CURRENT (v2) version rather than c3's own historical v1, and the two modern lua.unit_ai/lua.building_spawn components are replaced by a single, EMPTY opaque 'lua-state' component (the pre-#761 shape), all descriptors marked required exactly as a genuine #760 writer always did. Proves World.Save.Envelope.decodeB2SessionEnvelope's new fallback (tried after the modern path fails on the missing modern Lua ids, and after the B1 fallback defers since this isn't {metadata,session}) reassembles every Haskell component through the SAME real registry/ccInputVers dispatch the modern path uses, defaulting current Lua modules the same way a migrated B1 session does (isMigratedLegacyBaseline=true, luaComponentCount=0) since the lua-state blob is empty.
